content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND for autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new data samples and environmental experiences are stored in an active learning agent’s long-term memory, creating a self-organizing memory system that automatically retains only high-uncertainty, information-rich content aligned with the agent’s ongoing learning goals. This eliminates the need for hand-designed memory pruning rules that often discard unexpectedly valuable data.
